title: relay.rs: custom reqwest client lacks SsrfSafeResolver, enabling DNS rebinding
description:
LOCATION: src/relay.rs:65-92, relay::add()

FINDING: relay::add() performs an SSRF check on relay_url (the actor URL) and on inbox_uri extracted from the actor document. However, the SSRF check on inbox_uri at lines 70-79 resolves the host via is_private_host_resolved(). Between this check and the actual HTTP POST to inbox_uri (lines 127-134), there is a TOCTOU window where DNS rebinding can redirect the hostname to a private IP. This is the same DNS rebinding gap that the SsrfSafeResolver was designed to close on the main http_client — but relay.rs builds its own reqwest::Client at line 42 WITHOUT the SsrfSafeResolver. The client built in relay::add() has no custom DNS resolver, so DNS rebinding attacks on relay subscription are not prevented at the TCP connect level.

IMPACT: An attacker who controls DNS for a relay hostname can serve a valid actor document during the is_private_host_resolved check, then rebind DNS to 127.0.0.1 between the check and the TCP connection, causing an HTTP POST to an internal service.
